teacher: Matsunami Goro (Hero name: 'Scaffold' — Quirk: Construct, builds rigid scaffolding structures from ambient minerals. Methodical, patient, and quietly competitive about his class.)
reputation: The class that almost made it to 1-A. Their entrance exam scores were comparable — in some technical categories, better. Matsunami runs a tighter ship than Oshiro. 1-B students know their fundamentals cold before they start improvising. Less chaos, more precision. They watch 1-A the way you watch someone doing something impressive badly.
sports_festival_note: Tier 2, Arc 1. Class 1-B enter the Sports Festival as the school's open secret — everyone's picked a 1-A favourite, nobody's thought hard about 1-B. That's exactly how Matsunami wants it. He has drilled his students on 1-A's quirks from the entrance exam footage. This is the arc where 1-B stops being background noise.
dm_overview: Introduce 1-B students gradually through Tier 1 — brief corridor encounters, overheard conversations, cafeteria sightings. None of them are villains. None of them are friends yet. They're rivals in the classical sense: people who make your students better by existing. By the time the Sports Festival starts, players should have a clear read on at least 3-4 of these students and be genuinely unsure who's going to win.

tier1_introduction_guide.arc1_entrance_exam: The results board after the exam shows Class 1-A and 1-B scores in adjacent columns. 1-B's top score (Morida: 84 pts) is visible. Whether it beats or matches 1-A's best depends on the session. Fujisaki mentions '1-B had a different site this year — quieter, but thorough.' Oshiro says nothing about 1-B. He doesn't need to.
tier1_introduction_guide.arc2_dorm_life: Morida and Yashiro are visible in the cafeteria. Morida's tray is geometrically arranged. He looks at the 1-A table once, says nothing. Yashiro catches the eye of whoever's watching and winks. The practice room booking shows 1-B has 6am slots every weekday. Ogiwara's warmup shakes the building at 7am. The class notices.
tier1_introduction_guide.arc3_capture_the_flag: Ogiwara is watching through the observation window. When a student looks over, he gives a slow thumbs up. It's impossible to tell if it's sincere or condescending. Oshiro drops the line: 'Class 1-B had three students who topped this score in their equivalent drill. That's your benchmark.'
tier1_introduction_guide.arc4_agency_visit: Kitamura Sora is at the same agency district for a different internship. If the class encounters her: she's carrying precisely folded documents, gives the impression she's been there three days, and has a 10-second corridor interaction before continuing. She doesn't introduce herself. Her name badge faces inward. If a student catches a glimpse: Kitamura, S., Class 1-B.

students.name: Morida Takeshi

quirk_mechanics.weakness: His hardened form can only absorb a limited amount of damage before it shatters, ending the effect and returning him to normal — any damage taken beyond his absorption limit carries over to his normal form.

abilities.name: Harden
abilities.description: Turns his skin to stone, gaining resistance to all damage types (except necrotic, psychic, and poison) for minutes equal to half his Constitution modifier. As a reaction, can move up to half his speed to intercept an incoming attack on an ally's behalf. Creatures within 10 ft directly behind him gain half cover.

abilities.name: Stone Weapon
abilities.description: Fists become blades (slashing) or solid stone fists (bludgeoning), dealing 1d10 + Strength modifier damage. Once per turn on a hit, deals bonus Xd4 damage (X = half Constitution modifier, rounded down). Gains +1 AC.

abilities.name: Re-Harden
abilities.description: If his Harden threshold is breached, regains 10 HP instantly; usable up to five times in one reaction. After using it, falls unconscious for rounds equal to the number of uses beyond the first. Once per short rest.

abilities.name: Unbreakable
abilities.description: Hardens his entire body for 4 rounds, becoming immune to all damage except necrotic, psychic, and poison, and granting total cover to allies directly behind him. Gains piercing damage and a climbing speed. Falls unconscious for 3 rounds and gains three levels of exhaustion afterward.

students.quirk_summary: Full-body metallic plating. Morida's skin hardens into dense, layered steel armour that he can extend or retract at will. At full hardening he's nearly impervious to physical attacks. At partial hardening he trades some protection for mobility. He cannot harden his eyes — his one consistent weak point.
students.personality: Disciplined, methodical, and quietly contemptuous of flashy quirk use. He respects results and is suspicious of anything he can't measure. Doesn't talk during a fight. Will compliment a student after if they earned it — which sounds like 'That was efficient.' He means it as high praise.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Engine vs Wall. Izuto's raw output vs Morida's near-absolute defence. The central question is whether Izuto can generate enough force to crack the armour before Morida just outlasts him. Morida has already figured out Izuto's timing. Izuto hasn't thought about Morida at all yet.
students.sports_festival_role: The unmovable object in direct contests. Morida's goal in every event is to simply not be eliminated. He doesn't need points — he needs to still be standing when everyone else isn't. He and Izuto will meet in the tournament bracket. That fight is the centrepiece of the Sports Festival arc.
students.tier1_appearance: Cafeteria during dorm move-in. Perfectly level tray. Makes eye contact with no one. Once, in the corridor, he passes Izuto without acknowledging him. Izuto doesn't notice. Morida does.
students.dm_notes: Morida is not a villain and not a bad person. He's the structural version of Masahata — someone who sees everything as a competition and is usually right about where everyone stands. Let him be correct. Let his correctness be slightly annoying. The payoff is at the Sports Festival when his certainty runs into Izuto's stubbornness.
signature_moves: Iron Stance: as a bonus action, increase AC by 2 until start of next turn (total AC 20). Speed drops to 0.
Armour Crash: full hardening bull rush — move 20ft in a line, +6 to hit, 2d8+3 bludgeoning, DC 14 STR save or knocked prone. Recharge 5-6.
Selective Hardening: can harden specific body parts — hardened fist deals +1d6 on a hit. Always on.

students.name: Yashiro Fumiko

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Part of her real body must be present to release the dark fog, exposing her to attack. Portals are coordinate-based, requiring her to know the exact location or have a clear line of sight, and she cannot create portals in heavy winds.

abilities.name: Portal Creation
abilities.description: Creates a number of 20-ft diameter portals up to her Proficiency Bonus within 30 feet, connecting to any location she can see or for which she has precise coordinates. Each portal lasts up to one minute or until dismissed. As a reaction, she can close a portal as a creature attempts to move through it — that creature makes a Dexterity save against her Quirk DC or takes 3d10 damage and is pushed back 10 feet.

abilities.name: Fog Shroud
abilities.description: Envelops herself in dark fog, becoming heavily obscured for one minute. While shrouded she can create portals as a bonus action, and attacks against her while obscured have disadvantage.

students.quirk_summary: Short-range teleportation. Yashiro can teleport up to 10 ft in any direction as a free action. Longer jumps (up to 30ft) require a bonus action and a 3-round cooldown. She can't blink through solid objects but can blink around corners she can see around. She uses this continuously and casually, which is deeply disorienting to fight.
students.personality: Smug, playful, treats combat like a game she's already won. Finds 1-A's chaos endearing in the way that people find puppies endearing — they're trying so hard. Genuinely likes competition. Would rather lose a good fight than win a boring one. Her competitive sincerity is the thing that catches people off guard — they expect cheap tricks and get instead a student who is actually, seriously fast.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Speed vs Evasion. Ren is faster in a straight line. Yashiro can't be chased in one. The question is whether Ren can close to a range where his attacks land before she blinks out. She's studied his Overclock pattern. He doesn't know she exists yet.
students.sports_festival_role: The worst person to be paired against in any containment event — she just isn't there. She's going to be in the tournament bracket and her match against Ren is the one that players will talk about afterwards regardless of who wins.
students.tier1_appearance: First spotted vanishing around a corner that didn't have a turn in it. If a student catches her eye in the cafeteria, she winks and blinks to three seats over without using the gap.
students.dm_notes: Yashiro is genuinely fun to run. She should feel slippery and quick, not cheap. There's always a clear solution to fighting her — the 3-round cooldown on long jumps, the can't-blink-through-solids rule — but finding it under pressure is hard. She and Ren actually like each other by the end of the Festival. Neither admits it.
signature_moves: Blink Step (Free): teleport up to 10 ft as part of movement. Can do this each turn without cost.
Long Jump (Bonus action): teleport up to 30ft. 3-round cooldown.
Phantom Strike: teleports adjacent to a target, attacks, then blinks 10ft away as part of the same action. Doesn't provoke opportunity attacks from that target.

students.name: Denma Sora

quirk_mechanics.weakness: His fingers must be exposed to use the Quirk, and prolonged use risks collateral damage — a failed save against Black Hole within 10 feet of fragile structures may cause them to collapse. While his fingers are exposed, ranged attacks against him within 10 feet have disadvantage and loose objects under 50 pounds drift toward him.

abilities.name: Black Hole
abilities.description: Creates a gravitational pulse in a 30-ft cone. Each creature makes a Strength save against his Quirk DC, taking 2d8 force damage and being pulled 15 feet closer on a fail, or half damage on a success. A creature ending its turn within 5 feet of him after a failed save becomes Restrained, taking 2d8 force damage at the start of each turn until it saves again.

abilities.name: Singularity Vortex
abilities.description: Creates a miniature black hole at a point within 60 feet for 1 minute. Each creature starting its turn within 15 feet makes a Strength save or is pulled 10 feet closer and takes 6d8 force damage (half on success). The area is difficult terrain and ranged attacks through it have disadvantage. Can be moved up to 20 feet as a bonus action.

abilities.name: Micro Collapse
abilities.description: Can disintegrate up to 50 pounds of non-living material (metal, stone, wood) by focusing the black hole, useful for disarming or destroying small unattended items or weakened constructs.

students.quirk_summary: Creates localised gravitational anomalies. Denma can designate a 10ft sphere within 60ft as either a high-gravity zone (things pulled toward its centre) or a low-gravity zone (things float away from it). Maintaining a zone requires concentration. He can have two zones active simultaneously at full concentration, but cannot attack while doing so.
students.personality: Quiet. Unsettling. Speaks in short declarative sentences and rarely finishes thoughts that the other person can follow anyway. Not cold — just somewhere else most of the time. Will talk at length about gravitational physics and the difference between mass and weight in a way that is technically correct and socially obliterating. The moments where he's actually paying attention to a person are rare and feel significant.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Both manipulate the battlefield rather than dealing direct damage. Chozen builds stress, breaks people down. Denma controls movement, removes options. Their rivalry is philosophical more than physical — two students who win through mechanism rather than power. At the Festival: both will try to make the other fight on their terms. Neither will fully succeed.
students.sports_festival_role: The wildcard in every group event. His zones control the field in ways that benefit teammates and punish enemies. In the tournament bracket, he's dangerous specifically because he doesn't need to beat you — he just needs you to be in the wrong place. Almost everyone underestimates the range.
students.tier1_appearance: A practice room booking under 1-B's name. Inside: twelve thumbtacks floating 2mm off the board, perfectly static. No explanation. The room smells faintly of ozone.
students.dm_notes: Denma is designed to be memorable through strangeness rather than threat. His encounters in Tier 1 should be brief and odd — a glance, a sentence, something not quite right about gravity in his vicinity. Save his actual fighting capability for the Festival. He should feel like a puzzle that hasn't been asked yet.
signature_moves: Gravity Zone (Action): designate a 10ft sphere within 60ft as high-gravity (creatures pulled to centre, speed halved) or low-gravity (creatures float up 5ft, speed halved in different axis). Concentration, 1 minute.
Dual Field: maintain two zones simultaneously. Cannot attack or take bonus actions while sustaining both.
Crush Point: collapse a high-gravity zone onto a single target. DC 14 STR save or restrained for 1 round and takes 2d6 force damage. Destroys the zone.

students.name: Ogiwara Bunta

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Prolonged use strains his muscles and joints — after using Big Fist a number of times equal to his level times his proficiency bonus, he must make a DC 13 Constitution save or take 1d12 true damage.

abilities.name: Giant Strength
abilities.description: Strength score maximum becomes 22, and carrying capacity is doubled.

abilities.name: Giant Punch
abilities.description: Enlarges a hand and strikes all creatures in a 10-ft long, 10-ft wide line, dealing 2d12 bludgeoning damage and producing a wind shock that pushes targets back 15 feet.

abilities.name: Ground Pound
abilities.description: Slams an enlarged hand into the ground. All creatures within a 20-ft radius make a Dexterity save or take 4d12 bludgeoning damage and are knocked prone; the area becomes difficult terrain.

students.quirk_summary: Ogiwara can enlarge either hand to enormous size, vastly increasing his striking power and reach. A straight punch becomes a battering ram that flattens anything in a line; slammed into the ground it sends a shockwave of displaced air and debris radiating outward. Scaling: a standard Giant Punch hits everything in a 10-ft line. A full Ground Pound clears a 20-ft radius and leaves the area difficult terrain. Doesn't work if his hand can't reach the target or the ground.
students.personality: Loud. Genuinely, physically loud — his voice carries without him trying. Enthusiastic competitor in the way that makes it fun to lose to him because he's so happy about having a real contest. No subtlety. No hidden agenda. Will shake your hand and mean it. Will also absolutely try to put you through the floor.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Sound vs Big Fist. Both create broad-area chaos. Both are physically enormous and running at things is their default strategy. They'll either be best friends or destroy each other immediately at the Festival — the exact circumstances of their first meeting will determine which. Either outcome is correct.
students.sports_festival_role: Devastating in group events that require movement or terrain control. In the tournament bracket, he's the crowd favourite — his Giant Punch and Ground Pound create visible, dramatic shockwaves that the audience loves. Weakness: if someone gets him airborne or out of arm's reach, his quirk is useless. Not a weakness he's addressed.
students.tier1_appearance: Two 7am shockwaves on move-in morning, felt through the dorm floor. If confronted: 'Warmup.' Delivered as a complete explanation. Gives the thumbs up through the Capture the Flag observation window — entirely genuine, completely unreadable.
students.dm_notes: Ogiwara is the relief. Run every Tier 1 scene with him as a moment of levity. He has no secrets, no agenda, no hidden depth to excavate. His arc at the Sports Festival is uncomplicated: he wants to test himself against Toshida. Give him that fight and let it be excellent.
signature_moves: Giant Punch (Scaled): enlarges a hand and strikes everything in a 10-ft line. 2d10 bludgeoning damage and a wind-shock push of 10 feet.
Ground Pound (Scaled): slams an enlarged hand into the ground. 15-ft radius, 3d10 bludgeoning, DC 14 DEX save or knocked prone. Difficult terrain left behind. Recharge 5-6.
Giant Strength (Passive): his Strength score maximum is treated as 22, and his carrying capacity is doubled.

students.name: Nashiro Aoi

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Physical contact is required to copy a Quirk, and each copied power fades after two minutes. Accumulation- or mutation-based Quirks cannot be properly replicated, and upon copying she inherits all of its drawbacks, requiring a successful DC 16 Athletics check — on a failure, she cannot attempt to copy that specific Quirk again for seven rounds.

abilities.name: Mimic Touch
abilities.description: When she lands an unarmed strike on a creature, she may copy their Quirk. The copied Quirk lasts two minutes (three at 5th level, five at 10th level).

abilities.name: Quirk Swap
abilities.description: Switches between stored copied Quirks. She may maintain up to two copied Quirks at once, increasing by one additional Quirk at 5th and 10th level.

students.quirk_summary: Nashiro can borrow a Quirk by landing a hit on its user — for two minutes she has full use of whatever ability they're carrying, layered over (or instead of) her own approach to a fight. She's spent years cataloguing how other people's powers actually work in practice, which means she rarely copies something she doesn't already know how to use well. She cannot copy mutation- or accumulation-based Quirks, and copying anything risky means inheriting its drawbacks too.
students.personality: Theatrical. Observant in the way that only people who spend a lot of time watching other people are. In public she's performing — always, slightly, a version of herself edited for the room. In private (which almost never happens with 1-A) she's dry and tired in an interesting way. She's been reading Class 1-A since the results board, cataloguing every Quirk she's seen on tape and deciding which would actually be worth taking from them in a fight.
students.rivalry_dynamic: The obvious play for Nashiro against Hellflame is to land a hit and copy it — Kazama knows this, which means the real fight is about whether she can tag him before he adjusts his approach to deny her the touch. If she copies his fire, the fight changes entirely on her terms. Kazama doesn't know she exists yet. Their Sports Festival match, if it happens, is about whether Nashiro can land that first hit before Kazama figures out what she's doing.
students.sports_festival_role: Nightmarish in group events that involve direct confrontation — she can absorb whoever hits her first and turn their own Quirk against the rest of the field. In the tournament bracket, she's a puzzle encounter: students need to avoid giving her a clean touch while she's actively hunting for the most dangerous Quirk on the field to borrow.
students.tier1_appearance: A 1-A student swears Hamagawa tagged them with a light shove in a sparring drill, then suddenly fought like Hamagawa for thirty seconds. It wasn't Hamagawa. Nobody can prove it was Nashiro. If directly confronted: 'I don't know what you're referring to.' She says it with Hamagawa's exact dry cadence.
students.dm_notes: Nashiro is a slow-burn reveal. Her Tier 1 appearances should all be deniable — a tagged opponent briefly fighting unlike themselves, easy to write off as imagination. By the Sports Festival, players should be actively wary of letting her land a hit, which is exactly where she wants them.
signature_moves: Mimic Touch (Bonus action): on landing an unarmed strike, copy the target's Quirk. Lasts two minutes. Cannot copy mutation- or accumulation-based Quirks, and inherits the copied Quirk's drawbacks.
Quirk Swap (Bonus action): switches which copied Quirk is currently active. Can hold up to two copied Quirks in reserve at once.
Cold Read (Passive): from prior observation of an opponent's Quirk in use, she has advantage on the Athletics check required when first copying it.

students.name: Kitamura Sora

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Tape is durable but not unbreakable. She can produce tape for up to 10 minutes of use; after that, she cannot generate more until she takes a short rest to recover from the resulting dry skin.

abilities.name: Grapple and Restrain
abilities.description: Shoots tape at a target within 60 feet. On a hit, the target becomes grappled and restrained, with a Strength save against her Quirk DC at the end of its turn to break free. While grappled, she can move the creature left or right up to 30 feet.

abilities.name: Throwing Objects
abilities.description: Attaches tape to up to two objects and throws them, making two separate attacks — each object deals bludgeoning damage based on its size per the Object Size table (e.g., Medium objects 1d8, Large objects 2d8).

abilities.name: Rescue Maneuver
abilities.description: Shoots tape at a grappled ally, pulling them toward her and allowing her to move up to 60 feet with them to reposition or avoid attacks.

abilities.name: Barricade Tape
abilities.description: Rapidly ejects tape to cover up to a 10-ft square radius 60 feet away, creating a sticky barrier that provides half cover to allies and halves the speed of creatures moving through it.

students.quirk_summary: Generates strong, adhesive tape from her forearms. She can fire it to bind a target at range, sling tape-wrapped objects as improvised weapons, throw a tether to pull an ally clear of danger, or flood an area with sticky barricade tape that bogs down anyone moving through it. Setting up a barricade or restraint line in a room takes her approximately six seconds. She has timed this.
students.personality: Precise. Economical. Considers Class 1-A's operational style inefficient in the way an architect considers a building that keeps falling down. She isn't contemptuous — she's concerned, professionally. Has extremely strong opinions about workspace organisation and does not volunteer them unsolicited. Will volunteer them if asked. Has very few opinions about things she considers not her domain. Says 'Yes.' and 'No.' as complete sentences.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Size vs Tape. Nakaya's enlargement makes her a large target — and a large target takes a lot more tape to bring down, but once Kitamura's got enough of it on her, the restraint scales with the problem. Nakaya's advantage is that at sufficient size, she can simply tear through tape that would hold a normal-sized opponent — but that takes time and effort Kitamura can use against her. Their rivalry is about Nakaya learning that raw scale has hard limits.
students.sports_festival_role: Devastating in any event with structure — she pre-rigs environments with barricade tape during setup phases. In group contests, she's invisible until suddenly four students are bound simultaneously. In the tournament bracket, she's the student everyone is afraid to face because they've seen her work and don't know where the tape is.
students.tier1_appearance: Corridor encounter during the Agency Visit arc. 10 seconds. She's carrying precisely folded documents, doesn't introduce herself, continues walking. Her name badge faces inward. If a student catches a glimpse: Kitamura, S., 1-B. The practice room she used before leaving has tape patterns still strung across it at odd angles — easy to miss until someone almost walks into one.
students.dm_notes: Kitamura is the student who makes players feel like they've been outplayed by something they didn't see coming. Her Tier 1 presence should be minimal and precise — the corridor encounter, the tape patterns. Nothing else. By the Sports Festival, her reputation has been built entirely by absence. The fight against her should be the one that makes players think hard about the environment before they enter it.
signature_moves: Barricade Tape (Reaction): ejects tape to cover a 10-ft square up to 60ft away, creating a sticky barrier that grants allies half cover and halves the speed of creatures moving through it.
Grapple and Restrain (Action): shoots tape at a target within 60ft. On a hit, the target is grappled and restrained, with a Strength save at the end of its turn to break free.
Rescue Maneuver (Reaction): shoots tape at a grappled ally, pulling them toward her and repositioning up to 60 feet to get them clear of danger.

students.name: Hatsuda Renko

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Sustained use builds a static charge in her own body — after three rounds of continuous Electrification, she takes 1d6 lightning damage at the start of her turn until she stops for a round to discharge safely.

abilities.name: Charged Strikes
abilities.description: Unarmed strikes deal an additional 1d6 lightning damage.

abilities.name: Conductive Surge
abilities.description: Touches a conductive surface or creature, sending a surge through it. Each creature in contact with the same surface within 30 feet makes a Constitution save against her Quirk DC, taking 3d6 lightning damage and being stunned until the end of their next turn on a fail, or half damage on a success.

abilities.name: Static Field
abilities.description: Surrounds herself with a crackling field for one minute. Any creature that hits her with a melee attack takes 1d8 lightning damage. Metal weapons used against her have disadvantage.

students.quirk_summary: Hatsuda generates and channels electrical current through her skin. She's most dangerous through contact — a touch, a handshake, a held railing — and the entire dorm building's plumbing taught her this the hard way during move-in week. She can't discharge through the air at range; everything she does requires a conductive bridge, which means she spends a lot of time thinking three steps ahead about what's metal in the room.
students.personality: Restless, fidgety, talks fast when she's nervous and faster when she's not. Treats every space she enters as a tactical map of conductive surfaces — railings, lockers, puddles — without really meaning to. Funny in a way that lands half the time. The other half she's already moved on to the next thought before anyone's caught up.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Water Gun vs Electrification is the worst possible matchup for one of them and Hatsuda has already worked out which. Masahata's water becomes a conductor the instant she touches it — his own quirk turns into her delivery system. He hasn't realised this yet. She has, and is saving it.
students.sports_festival_role: Lethal in any event involving water, metal terrain, or grouped opponents in contact with the same surface. In the tournament bracket, the smart play against her is to stay on dry, non-conductive ground — most opponents don't think to ask before the match starts.
students.tier1_appearance: A 1-A student grabs a stair railing during a fire drill and gets a static shock sharp enough to swear about. Hatsuda is three floors up, not even looking, and apologises without sounding sorry.
students.dm_notes: Hatsuda's threat is entirely about terrain awareness. Tier 1 should establish that she's always cataloguing what's conductive in a room. Her match against Masahata at the Festival should hinge on whether he realises what his own water is doing before it's too late.
signature_moves: Charged Strikes (Passive): unarmed strikes deal an extra 1d6 lightning damage.
Conductive Surge (Action): touches a conductive surface; everyone touching it within 30 feet makes a CON save or takes 3d6 lightning damage and is stunned.
Static Field (Bonus Action): for one minute, melee attackers take 1d8 lightning damage on a hit against her.

students.name: Fujikawa Renta

quirk_mechanics.weakness: His body's mass increases significantly while steeled, reducing his speed by 10 feet and making him vulnerable to forced movement effects which deal an extra 1d6 damage to him on a failed save.

abilities.name: Steel Body
abilities.description: Transforms his body into steel for one minute. Gains resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage, advantage on Strength checks and saves, but speed is reduced by 10 feet.

abilities.name: Steel Fist
abilities.description: Unarmed strikes deal 1d10 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage and count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance.

abilities.name: Immovable Stance
abilities.description: While in Steel Body, can brace against an incoming forced-movement or knockback effect, automatically succeeding the save against being moved, at the cost of his reaction.

students.quirk_summary: Fujikawa's entire body turns to dense, lustrous steel — heavier, slower, nearly impervious. Unlike Morida's stone hardening, his transformation is total and can't be partial; it's all or nothing, and once he's steeled, he's committed to the trade-off of speed for permanence. He treats this as an honest deal rather than a flaw.
students.personality: Stoic to the point of being mistaken for boring, which he finds mildly amusing. Has an old-fashioned sense of duty about being the class's anchor in group events. Doesn't volunteer opinions about other people's quirks but will defend Morida's methods to anyone who calls 1-B's style 'rigid.' Plays a lot of shogi against himself between classes.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Vines vs Steel is patience against patience — Kibi can bind and constrict, but binding something that doesn't need to breathe or bend buys Fujikawa time he's happy to spend. The fight, if it happens, is a long one and neither of them minds that.
students.sports_festival_role: An anchor in any event requiring someone to hold a position or absorb sustained pressure. Slow enough that speed-based opponents can simply outrun him — a gap he knows about and has made peace with rather than solved.
students.tier1_appearance: Seen in the practice yard standing motionless in full Steel Body for the full one-minute duration, timing himself with a stopwatch held in a normal hand he didn't bother to steel.
students.dm_notes: Fujikawa is a straightforward wall-archetype with one clean exploitable weakness — speed. Use him as a teaching encounter for kiting tactics. His rivalry with Kibi should be a slow, almost friendly war of attrition rather than a dramatic clash.
signature_moves: Steel Body (Bonus Action): transforms for one minute. Resistance to bludgeoning/piercing/slashing, advantage on STR checks/saves, speed -10 ft.
Steel Fist: while steeled, unarmed strikes deal 1d10 + STR bludgeoning damage.
Immovable Stance (Reaction): while steeled, automatically succeeds a save against forced movement.

students.name: Soga Mitsuru

quirk_mechanics.weakness: While phased, he cannot interact with the physical world at all — no attacks, no items, no allies. If a solid object closes around the space he's phasing through and he ends his phase inside it, he takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage and is shunted to the nearest open space.

abilities.name: Phase
abilities.description: Becomes incorporeal for up to one minute, able to move through solid objects and terrain at normal speed. Immune to non-magical damage while phased but cannot affect the physical world.

abilities.name: Partial Phase
abilities.description: Phases only an arm or leg, allowing him to reach through walls or floors to grab, retrieve, or strike — counts as an unarmed strike that ignores cover from the intervening barrier.

students.quirk_summary: Soga can render his body partially or fully intangible, passing through walls, floors, and most solid matter. Full Phase makes him untouchable but also unable to act on anything around him — he's a ghost for as long as it lasts. Partial Phase is the practical version: reach a hand through a wall, grab something, pull it back, all without ever being seen doing it.
students.personality: Quiet, watchful, faintly amused by how much other people worry about doors. Doesn't see the point of confrontation when he can simply not be where the confrontation is. This makes him hard to provoke and harder to actually fight. Has a dry running commentary he mostly keeps to himself.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Iron Claws vs Permeation. Aikawa hits hard and fast in close range — exactly the range where Soga simply isn't solid. The entire fight is Aikawa learning that committing to a strike against someone who can opt out of being hit is its own kind of trap, and Soga learning that eventually he has to solidify to do anything back.
students.sports_festival_role: A nightmare for capture or containment events — there's no cage that holds him. In the tournament bracket, his weakness is exposed the moment he needs to actually attack: he has to drop the phase, and that's the window.
students.tier1_appearance: A locked equipment closet in the gym is found open from the inside with no sign of forced entry. Soga is seen walking out of an adjacent hallway a minute later, looking unbothered.
students.dm_notes: Soga should feel genuinely unfair in defense and genuinely limited in offense — that tension is the whole character. Don't let him solo a fight; the moment he commits to hitting something, he's vulnerable, and smart players should learn to bait that moment.
signature_moves: Phase (Action): becomes incorporeal for up to one minute. Immune to non-magical damage, cannot affect the physical world.
Partial Phase (Bonus Action): phases an arm or leg to reach through a barrier — counts as an unarmed strike ignoring cover from that barrier.
Walk Through Walls (Passive, while phased): moves through solid terrain at normal speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

students.name: Anrakuji Hisame

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Visions are brief and disorienting — after using her Quirk, she suffers disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks until the end of her next turn, as the vision overlays her real sight.

abilities.name: Flash Foresight
abilities.description: When a creature within 30 feet makes an attack roll, she may grant herself or an ally within 30 feet a glimpse of the outcome — that creature gains advantage on its next saving throw or AC check against that specific attack.

abilities.name: Path Sight
abilities.description: Concentrates briefly to glimpse several seconds into the immediate future of a single creature within 60 feet, learning the general nature of its next action (attack, movement, ability use) before it happens. Usable a number of times equal to her proficiency bonus per long rest.

students.quirk_summary: Anrakuji catches brief, fragmentary glimpses of what's about to happen — not full futures, just a few seconds, usually centred on one person at a time. It's unreliable as a fortune-telling trick and devastating as a combat read. She's learned not to trust it for big decisions and to trust it completely for the next six seconds.
students.personality: Calm in a way that unsettles people who don't know why. Speaks carefully, like she's already heard the conversation once. Genuinely warm to people she trusts, which is a short list and entirely intentional. Hates being asked 'so what happens next' as a party trick — it doesn't work that way and she's tired of explaining it.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Iron Ball vs Foresight. Setaka's attacks are heavy, telegraphed, and devastating if they land — exactly the kind of thing Anrakuji's glimpses are built to dodge. The real contest is whether Setaka can vary his pattern enough that her foresight stops being useful before the fight's decided.
students.sports_festival_role: An incredible support piece in team events — warning allies a half-second before a hit lands changes outcomes constantly. In the tournament bracket, she's vulnerable to anyone whose attacks are genuinely unpredictable even to herself a few seconds out, which is rare but not impossible.
students.tier1_appearance: She tells a classmate to move a half-second before a dropped supply crate would have hit them. Doesn't explain. They thank her anyway, more confused than grateful.
students.dm_notes: Anrakuji is best run as a constant low-grade advantage rather than a flashy one — small warnings, redirected attacks, near-misses that look like luck. Keep her vision limited (seconds, not plans) so she never feels like she's solved the encounter in advance.
signature_moves: Flash Foresight (Reaction): when a creature within 30ft makes an attack roll, grant advantage on the next save/AC check against that attack to herself or an ally.
Path Sight (Action): glimpse a creature's next action a few seconds early. Limited uses per long rest.
Borrowed Half-Second (Passive): advantage on initiative rolls.

students.name: Karube Genta

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Augmentation must build over several rounds and cannot be triggered instantly — at the start of combat he is at his weakest, and abandoning a build-up (by being stunned or losing concentration) resets it to zero.

abilities.name: Building Power
abilities.description: At the start of each of his turns in combat, gains a stacking +1 bonus to Strength-based attack and damage rolls, to a maximum of +5 after five rounds.

abilities.name: Overdrive Release
abilities.description: Expends all current Building Power stacks in a single attack, adding the full stacked bonus as additional damage on a hit, then resets stacks to zero.

students.quirk_summary: Karube's muscles compound their own output the longer a fight runs — slow to start, terrifying by round five. The trade-off is brutal: anything that resets his momentum (a stun, a hard knockdown, a long pause) sends him back to square one. Fights against him are fights against a clock he's trying to survive long enough to win.
students.personality: Patient to a degree that frustrates faster classmates. Treats early-fight setbacks with total calm because he knows what round five looks like. Doesn't trash-talk. Will, however, very quietly count out loud when he's winning, which everyone finds more unnerving than any taunt.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Dupli-Arms vs Muscle Augmentation. Samitsu's extra limbs let him overwhelm early with sheer action economy — precisely the window where Karube is weakest. If Samitsu can't close it out fast, round five arrives and the math flips hard in Karube's favour.
students.sports_festival_role: A slow-burn threat in any extended bout — the longer an event runs, the worse a proposition he becomes. In short, fast events he's mediocre, which the entrance exam scores reflected and which 1-A has no reason to suspect changes everything by round five.
students.tier1_appearance: Sparring drill footage shows him losing badly for the first four rounds and then ending it in two hits. Nobody who saw only the first half believes the second half happened.
students.dm_notes: Karube rewards players who either end fights fast or specifically target his concentration to reset his stacks. Telegraph the build clearly (describe the visible escalation each round) so the threat feels earned rather than arbitrary.
signature_moves: Building Power (Passive): +1 to STR attack/damage rolls at the start of each turn in combat, stacking to +5.
Overdrive Release (Action): spends all stacks on one attack as bonus damage, then resets to zero.
Steady Pace (Passive): immune to being frightened; resets to zero on a failed concentration check instead of automatic loss.

students.name: Ezumi Wakana

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Her edges are fragile against anything harder than flesh — striking a hardened or armoured surface with a Razor Strike forces a Constitution save or the edge dulls, losing the bonus damage until she takes an action to resharpen.

abilities.name: Razor Strike
abilities.description: Unarmed strikes are treated as slashing and deal an additional 1d6 damage.

abilities.name: Whirling Edge
abilities.description: Spins rapidly, making a slashing attack against every creature within 5 feet, each taking 2d8 slashing damage on a hit.

abilities.name: Resharpen
abilities.description: Restores a dulled edge, removing the penalty from Razor Strike.

students.quirk_summary: Ezumi's skin grows razor-edged ridges along her forearms and shins that she can extend at will. Fast, precise, and brutal in close range, but the edges are surprisingly delicate against anything armoured — she's learned to read what she's about to hit before she commits to hitting it.
students.personality: Sharp-tongued in exactly the way you'd expect, and aware enough of the joke to be tired of making it herself. Competitive in short bursts, then disinterested once she's proven her point. Genuinely kind to people who aren't currently in a fight with her.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Scales vs Razor Sharp. Ando's natural armour is exactly the surface that dulls her edges fastest — she has to find the gaps in his scales rather than meeting him head-on, which goes against every instinct she has for closing a fight quickly.
students.sports_festival_role: Devastating against unarmoured opponents in close-quarters events, considerably less so against anything hardened or plated. In the tournament bracket, her matchups are extremely lopsided in one direction or the other.
students.tier1_appearance: A training dummy in the gym is found sliced clean through at a diagonal. The instructor says nothing, just adds another dummy to the supply order.
students.dm_notes: Ezumi is a glass-cannon against soft targets and a genuine puzzle against armoured ones. Lean into the read-your-opponent angle — let her hesitate visibly against Morida or Fujikawa-type targets, and go all-in against anyone unarmoured.
signature_moves: Razor Strike (Passive): unarmed strikes deal an extra 1d6 slashing damage.
Whirling Edge (Action): slashing attack against everything within 5 feet, 2d8 damage each on a hit.
Resharpen (Action): removes the dulled-edge penalty after striking a hard surface.

students.name: Tsukikage Renji

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Generating a bright flash leaves his own eyes unprotected — anyone he blinds with Flash Burst also forces him to make the same Constitution save, or he is blinded alongside them until the end of his next turn.

abilities.name: Flash Burst
abilities.description: Emits a blinding pulse of light in a 20-ft radius. Each creature in the area, including himself, makes a Constitution save against his Quirk DC or is blinded until the end of its next turn.

abilities.name: Guiding Light
abilities.description: Emits a soft, controlled glow that illuminates a 30-ft radius for one minute, and grants advantage on the next attack roll made by an ally who can see the light against a target within it.

abilities.name: Dazzle Step
abilities.description: When targeted by an attack, emits a localized flash at the attacker only, forcing a Constitution save or the attack is made with disadvantage.

students.quirk_summary: Tsukikage's body produces light at will, from a soft ambient glow to a blinding pulse bright enough to whiteout a room. He's spent more time than anyone admits learning to control the brightness curve so he doesn't blind himself every single time, with mixed success.
students.personality: Easygoing, a little dramatic, treats his own quirk like a stage light he gets to operate. Enjoys being the centre of attention in a literal sense and is fine with it being figurative too. Surprisingly tactical when it matters — the showmanship is real but it's not the whole picture.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Double vs Luminescence. Yoshibara's duplicate is visually identical to her — right up until Tsukikage floods the area with light bright enough to expose any tell, a shadow that falls wrong, a half-second of hesitation the real one doesn't have. The fight is about whether she can act fast enough that it doesn't matter which one he's looking at.
students.sports_festival_role: Excellent in night or visibility-based events, and useful in any group contest as a battlefield-wide debuff. In the tournament bracket, his own blind-spot weakness gives sighted, fast opponents a real window if they're willing to eat the flash to get it.
students.tier1_appearance: The dorm hallway lights flicker out during a blackout drill and Tsukikage is visible glowing faintly at the end of the corridor, entirely unbothered, reading by his own light.
students.dm_notes: Tsukikage's blindness-on-himself clause is the built-in balance lever — make sure players learn it exists, ideally by him blinding himself at an inconvenient moment once in Tier 1, so it reads as established rather than a Festival-only reveal.
signature_moves: Flash Burst (Action): 20-ft radius blinding pulse. Everyone inside, including himself, makes a CON save or is blinded.
Guiding Light (Bonus Action): illuminates 30 ft for one minute, granting an ally advantage on their next attack against a target in the light.
Dazzle Step (Reaction): flashes an attacker targeting him, forcing a CON save or disadvantage on their attack.

students.name: Onogawa Subaru

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Vibration travels through whatever he's touching — using it while grappled or restrained deals equal damage back to himself, and he cannot use it underwater without the effect dissipating harmlessly.

abilities.name: Resonant Touch
abilities.description: On contact with a creature or object, channels high-frequency vibration. A struck creature takes 2d10 force damage and must make a Constitution save or be deafened for one minute. Objects take double damage from this effect for the purpose of breaking them.

abilities.name: Standing Wave
abilities.description: Sends vibration through the ground in a 15-ft radius centered on himself. Creatures in the area make a Dexterity save or are knocked prone and take 2d8 force damage.

students.quirk_summary: Onogawa can generate intense vibration through any surface he's in contact with — a touch that shatters glass, a floor-slam that drops everyone standing nearby. It's contact-based and surface-based, which means he's constantly thinking about footing and what he's touching, much like Hatsuda but for an entirely different reason.
students.personality: Blunt, a little gruff, says exactly what he thinks and is occasionally surprised when that lands badly. Respects effort over results, which makes him an easy training partner and a hard one to actually beat in his own estimation. Likes things that make satisfying noises.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Tail vs Vibrate. Isokono's tail gives him reach and balance Onogawa doesn't have — but every time that tail makes contact to strike, grapple, or brace, it becomes a conduit. The fight comes down to whether Isokono can fight Onogawa without ever letting the tail touch him directly.
students.sports_festival_role: Strong in terrain-destruction events and group events on shared surfaces — a single Standing Wave can take out several opponents sharing solid ground. In the tournament bracket, opponents who stay airborne or off solid contact neutralise him almost entirely.
students.tier1_appearance: A vending machine in the common room develops a hairline crack across the glass after Onogawa leans on it the wrong way during an argument. He pays for it without being asked.
students.dm_notes: Onogawa's vulnerability — underwater and aerial opponents — should be discoverable rather than announced. Let a smart player figure it out mid-fight. His rivalry with Isokono should hinge on tail-contact as the explicit fail condition for Isokono's usual strategy.
signature_moves: Resonant Touch (Action): on contact, 2d10 force damage and a CON save or deafened for one minute. Double damage to objects.
Standing Wave (Action): 15-ft radius ground slam, DC 14 DEX save or knocked prone and 2d8 force damage.
Shatterpoint (Passive): vibration damage ignores resistance granted purely by an object's hardness (e.g. glass, stone).

students.name: Mizuhana Kotori

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Effects are localized and require concentration; if her concentration breaks, the weather effect dissipates over the following round rather than ending instantly, sometimes inconveniencing her own allies before it clears.

abilities.name: Localized Squall
abilities.description: Conjures a 20-ft radius zone of heavy wind and rain within 60 feet for up to one minute (concentration). The area is lightly obscured, ranged attacks through it have disadvantage, and difficult terrain applies to anyone moving against the wind direction.

abilities.name: Static Charge
abilities.description: Within an active Localized Squall, can call down a lightning strike on a point within the zone. Each creature within 10 feet of the point makes a Dexterity save, taking 4d8 lightning damage on a fail or half on a success.

students.quirk_summary: Mizuhana can summon small, localized weather systems — wind, rain, and at higher intensity, lightning — centered on a point she can see. It's slow to build and entirely dependent on her staying focused, but a fully formed squall changes the terms of an entire fight, not just her part of it.
students.personality: Dreamy, unhurried, talks about weather the way other people talk about close friends. Surprisingly tactical underneath the soft exterior — she's run the numbers on exactly how much wind it takes to ruin a ranged attacker's day. Doesn't rattle easily; rain was never going to listen to her shouting at it, so she stopped shouting at anything.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Invisibility vs Weather Manipulation. Takazuki's biggest asset is not being seen — Mizuhana's storm fills the air with visible rain and wind-blown debris that outlines anything moving through it, invisible or not. The fight is about whether Takazuki can stay out of the squall entirely, which is a smaller battlefield than usual.
students.sports_festival_role: Quietly one of the most dangerous students at the Festival in any outdoor group event — a squall reshapes the entire field, not just her immediate area. In the tournament bracket, her concentration requirement is the obvious target; break her focus and the storm folds.
students.tier1_appearance: The practice yard floods with unscheduled light rain for exactly four minutes during her lunch break. No weather warning was issued. She apologises to a groundskeeper, who has stopped asking questions about it.
students.dm_notes: Mizuhana works best as a slow-building threat — give the squall time to visibly form before it matters, then let it dominate the back half of an encounter. Her concentration dependency is the fair counterplay; telegraph it so players learn to look for the opening.
signature_moves: Localized Squall (Action, concentration): 20-ft radius wind and rain zone for up to one minute. Lightly obscured, ranged disadvantage, difficult terrain against the wind.
Static Charge (Action, within an active Squall): lightning strike at a point; creatures within 10 ft make a DEX save, 4d8 lightning damage on a fail.
Eye of the Storm (Passive): she and allies within 5 feet of her are unaffected by her own Squall's obscurement and difficult terrain.

students.name: Hirosegawa Daichi

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Can only affect one target at a time and must maintain line of sight and concentration — if he loses sight of the target or his concentration breaks, the effect ends immediately.

abilities.name: Anchor Gaze
abilities.description: Targets one creature within 60 feet he can see. That creature makes a Wisdom save against his Quirk DC or has its speed reduced to 0 for as long as he maintains concentration (up to one minute). The target may repeat the save at the end of each of its turns.

abilities.name: Total Lock
abilities.description: As Anchor Gaze, but on a failed save the target is also restrained, and disadvantage applies to its repeated saves while he maintains concentration.

students.quirk_summary: Hirosegawa can fix a single target's position in place through sustained eye contact — not telekinesis, more like locally suspending their ability to move at all. It's a single-target lockdown, total commitment, no multitasking: he picks one person and that's the whole fight for him until it ends.
students.personality: Intense, focused to the point of unnerving, rarely blinks during a conversation that matters to him (which is most of them, as far as anyone can tell). Doesn't do small talk well. Genuinely cares about his classmates in a way that comes out as overprotective vigilance rather than warmth.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Poltergeist vs Lock Down. Nomi doesn't need to move to be dangerous — she can hurl objects around the battlefield without her own body going anywhere, which makes locking down her movement almost beside the point. Hirosegawa's whole strategy needs a rethink against her, and he knows it, which makes this the rivalry he respects most and trusts least.
students.sports_festival_role: A precision tool against single dangerous targets — exactly what you want against a Festival favourite, exactly useless against anyone who fights through telekinesis or ranged effects rather than movement. In the tournament bracket, he's the student most likely to be intentionally paired against whoever the bracket considers the biggest threat.
students.tier1_appearance: A 1-A student in a hallway suddenly can't take a step for about four seconds, looks around in confusion, and Hirosegawa is already walking away by the time they figure out who did it.
students.dm_notes: Hirosegawa is a single-target control specialist; he should feel oppressive against one enemy and irrelevant against everyone else nearby. His rivalry with Nomi is conceptual — a control specialist meeting the one archetype his control doesn't really touch.
signature_moves: Anchor Gaze (Action, concentration): target within 60 ft makes a WIS save or speed becomes 0 until it saves again at the end of its turn.
Total Lock (Action, concentration): as Anchor Gaze, but also restrains the target and gives disadvantage on its repeated saves.
Unblinking (Passive): advantage on checks/saves to maintain concentration against effects that would force him to look away.

students.name: Natsukawa Riho

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Can only fold flat, non-living materials — anything thicker than a few inches or anything organic resists entirely, and over-folding a single object beyond its limit causes it to tear or break permanently.

abilities.name: Origami Construct
abilities.description: Folds a flat material (paper, thin metal sheet, cloth) within reach into a rigid construct — a blade, a shield, a glider wing — that functions mechanically as that item for one hour or until unfolded.

abilities.name: Rapid Refold
abilities.description: Unfolds and refolds an existing Origami Construct into a different shape without needing a fresh action.

students.quirk_summary: Natsukawa can fold any flat, non-living material into rigid, functional shapes — a sheet of paper into a blade sharp enough to cut, a tarp into a glider, a length of foil into a makeshift shield. The constructs hold their shape and mechanical function for about an hour before the material's own structure starts giving out. She's spent years getting genuinely good at this in a way that looks like a parlor trick until it isn't.
students.personality: Meticulous, a little precious about her materials, will absolutely lecture you about the correct way to store origami paper. Quietly proud of a craft that most people dismiss as cute. Funny about it in a self-aware way — she knows exactly how this looks and has stopped caring what people think.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Hologram vs Fold. Both build things that aren't what they first appear to be — Ichitaka's projections trick the eye, Natsukawa's constructs are real and tangible the moment you touch them. The fight is a question of whether Ichitaka's illusions can bait Natsukawa into wasting a fold on something that turns out to be fake.
students.sports_festival_role: Versatile and resourceful in scavenger or obstacle-based events — give her materials and she'll build a solution. In the tournament bracket, she's at a disadvantage against opponents who deny her any flat material to work with, which is rare but a real counter if anyone thinks of it.
students.tier1_appearance: A paper crane left on a 1-A student's desk turns out, on close inspection, to have a folded edge sharp enough to have actually cut the desk slightly. Nobody saw her do it.
students.dm_notes: Natsukawa rewards creative DMing — let players hand her unusual materials mid-encounter and have her improvise constructs from them. Her limitation (flat, non-living, time-limited) keeps her grounded; don't let her fold anything thick or organic.
signature_moves: Origami Construct (Action): folds flat material into a functional weapon, shield, or tool for one hour.
Rapid Refold (Bonus Action): reshapes an existing construct into a different one instantly.
Paper Edge (Passive): folded blade constructs deal 1d6 slashing damage and ignore the first point of an opponent's natural armor bonus.

students.name: Kuromine Sosuke

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Cords must physically connect to a surface or creature to transmit a heartbeat shockwave, and severing or removing a connected cord ends the effect immediately. He has a limited number of cords (six) and damaged cords don't regenerate until a long rest.

abilities.name: Jack In
abilities.description: Extends a retractable cord from his earlobes up to 30 feet, attaching it to a surface or creature. Once attached, the cord transmits an amplified heartbeat pulse — an attached creature makes a Constitution save or takes 2d8 thunder damage and is pushed 5 feet away from the source.

abilities.name: Network Pulse
abilities.description: If multiple cords are currently attached, sends a synchronized pulse through all of them simultaneously, each connected creature taking the Jack In damage again without a new attachment roll.

students.quirk_summary: Kuromine's earlobes extend into retractable, jack-like cords that can attach to surfaces or people and transmit an amplified shockwave pulse — used for both offense and improvised binding/climbing. He thinks about cord management the way Kitamura thinks about tape, and the two of them have had exactly one very long, very nerdy conversation about logistics that neither will admit to enjoying as much as they did.
students.personality: Cool and a little aloof on the surface, genuinely enthusiastic underneath about anything mechanical or rhythmic. Listens more than he talks. Has a habit of tapping a beat against his own leg when he's thinking, which classmates have learned means don't interrupt yet.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Twin Impact vs Earphone Jack. Toragi's twin-strike rhythm and Kuromine's cord network are both about timing and connection points — the fight comes down to whose rhythm the other can disrupt first. Kuromine has been quietly tracking Toragi's strike cadence from footage for weeks.
students.sports_festival_role: Strong in events with multiple simultaneous targets or grapple-and-control objectives — Network Pulse rewards setting up several connections before committing. In the tournament bracket, severing or avoiding his cords entirely shuts him down fast, which is a known, learnable counter.
students.tier1_appearance: A length of cord is found looped neatly around a stair railing in the dorm, clearly used to test grip strength, just as neatly retrieved before anyone asks who left it.
students.dm_notes: Kuromine plays like a setup specialist — give him a round or two to attach cords before Network Pulse becomes available, and let smart players sever or dodge the cords to deny him the payoff. His friendship-adjacent rivalry-of-craft with Kitamura is a nice beat to use in a shared scene if both classes cross paths.
signature_moves: Jack In (Action): extends a cord up to 30 ft to attach to a target. CON save or 2d8 thunder damage and pushed 5 feet.
Network Pulse (Action): re-triggers Jack In's damage on every currently attached creature at once.
Six Cords (Passive): can have up to six cords attached simultaneously; severing a cord requires an attack roll against it (AC 12).

students.name: Wakatabi Senri

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Redirected force must go somewhere — if there is no valid open direction to send it, the recoil instead deals half the absorbed damage to her directly, even though the original attack was negated.

abilities.name: Absorb and Redirect
abilities.description: When hit by an attack, can negate all damage from it and instead redirect the force as a burst in a direction of her choice within 15 feet, dealing the same damage to anything in a 5-ft-wide line in that direction.

abilities.name: Stored Recoil
abilities.description: Can hold up to two instances of absorbed force in reserve, releasing them on a later turn as a single combined burst instead of immediately.

students.quirk_summary: Wakatabi can absorb the force of an attack that hits her and immediately redirect it outward as a kinetic blast in a direction of her choosing — she doesn't take the hit, something else does. It only works reactively, on attacks that actually land, which means she has to be willing to get hit on purpose more often than most people would like.
students.personality: Fearless in a slightly worrying way — getting hit is half her kit, so she stops flinching from it long before her classmates do. Blunt, encouraging, the person who tells you to take the hit and trust the plan. People do, mostly because it's worked so far.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Neutralization vs Impact Recoil. Kinosho can shut down a quirk on contact — and Wakatabi's entire kit depends on her quirk being active the moment she's hit. If Kinosho lands the neutralizing touch first, Wakatabi just takes the damage like anyone else. The whole fight is a race to see who touches whom in what order.
students.sports_festival_role: Punishing in any event where opponents commit to a big single hit, since that hit becomes their problem redirected back at them or their allies. In the tournament bracket, opponents who use sustained, low-damage pressure rather than big single attacks deny her the leverage she needs.
students.tier1_appearance: A sparring partner's full-strength kick lands clean on Wakatabi and the practice dummy behind her explodes outward instead. The sparring partner checks their own foot, confused about why nothing hurts.
students.dm_notes: Wakatabi is a high-skill-expression reactive fighter — she's only as good as the attacks she's allowed to redirect. Her rivalry with Kinosho is a clean hard-counter dynamic; let that be the explicit tension if they ever meet on-screen.
signature_moves: Absorb and Redirect (Reaction): negate an attack's damage, redirect it as a 5-ft-wide line burst within 15 feet for equal damage.
Stored Recoil (Passive, 5th level): holds up to two absorbed instances in reserve to release as a combined burst later.
No Flinch (Passive): immune to being frightened by an attack she intends to absorb.

students.name: Ibarada Shun

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Touch is required to nullify gravity on a target, and the effect only lasts while he maintains concentration — releasing concentration drops the target immediately, from whatever height it's currently at.

abilities.name: Gravity Nullify
abilities.description: Touches a creature or object up to Large size, removing the effects of gravity on it for as long as he maintains concentration (up to one minute). The target floats and can be pushed in any direction with minimal force; it has no fall damage and cannot move under its own propulsion unless it has one.

abilities.name: Drop Cancel
abilities.description: When a creature affected by Gravity Nullify would take fall or impact damage immediately after the effect ends, halves that damage.

students.quirk_summary: Ibarada can touch a creature or object and remove its weight entirely, leaving it weightless and drifting until he lets go of the effect — at which point gravity reasserts itself all at once, wherever the target happens to be. He's quiet about how precisely he's worked out the timing on dropping someone from exactly the wrong height.
students.personality: Calm to the point of seeming detached, picks his moments rather than his fights. Doesn't enjoy conflict for its own sake but has a sharp, dry sense of when to act. Tends to be underestimated by people who don't realize how much of the fight is over the moment he gets a hand on you.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Dragon vs Zero Gravity. Tazato's draconic form is huge, strong, and built for aerial dominance — exactly the kind of mass and altitude Ibarada's quirk is designed to strip away. One clean touch and Tazato's biggest advantages, weight and flight control, both disappear at once. The trick is getting that touch on something that large and that dangerous.
students.sports_festival_role: A precision answer to any oversized or airborne opponent — genuinely frightening once he closes the distance. In the tournament bracket, the entire fight against him is about denying him that first touch, because everything after it goes his way.
students.tier1_appearance: A dropped textbook in the library hallway simply doesn't fall — it hangs at waist height for a few seconds before Ibarada, walking past without slowing, flicks two fingers and lets it drop the rest of the way into his own hand.
students.dm_notes: Ibarada is a control specialist whose entire threat profile hinges on landing one touch. Play him as patient and opportunistic rather than aggressive — he waits for the opening rather than forcing it. His rivalry with Tazato is the clean 'the bigger they are' dynamic.
signature_moves: Gravity Nullify (Action, concentration): touch a creature or object up to Large size, removing gravity's effect on it for up to one minute.
Drop Cancel (Reaction, 5th level): halves fall/impact damage when a Nullified target's weightlessness ends.
Light Touch (Passive): advantage on grapple checks made to establish the initial touch required for Gravity Nullify.
